title: Dizziness cardiac screening documentation

red_flags.text: Supports urgent cardiovascular assessment for ECG abnormality, heart failure, transient loss of consciousness during exertion, family history of sudden cardiac death, new or unexplained breathlessness, or a heart murmur.
red_flags.rationale: Cardiovascular red flags were reviewed as clinician-assessed prompts; escalation is never generated automatically.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports urgent cardiovascular assessment for ECG abnormality, heart failure, transient loss of consciousness during exertion, family history of sudden cardiac death, new or unexplained breathlessness, or a heart murmur.

source.title: Transient loss of consciousness ('blackouts') in over 16s

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.4.1–1.1.4.6 — red flags and urgent cardiovascular assessment

examination.text: Supports event and witness history, posture, prodrome, appearance, movements, tongue biting, injury, duration, confusion, focal weakness, previous events, medical and family history, medicines, vital signs, lying and standing blood pressure, cardiovascular examination, and neurological signs.
examination.rationale: The NICE section supports documenting event history, posture, prodrome, witness context, previous events, medicines, family history, vital signs, postural blood pressure, cardiovascular examination, and neurological features when dizziness includes transient loss of consciousness or a blackout concern.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports event and witness history, posture, prodrome, appearance, movements, tongue biting, injury, duration, confusion, focal weakness, previous events, medical and family history, medicines, vital signs, lying and standing blood pressure, cardiovascular examination, and neurological signs.

source.title: Transient loss of consciousness ('blackouts') in over 16s

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1.1–1.1.2.1 — initial history, witness account, examination, and vital signs

follow_up.text: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.
follow_up.rationale: The NICE AF section supports pulse, ECG, and ambulatory-monitoring context when dizziness raises clinician suspicion of AF; it does not diagnose AF.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1-1.1.3 - detection, 12-lead ECG, and ambulatory ECG

source_limitations: Dizziness phenotype, loss of consciousness, witness history, posture, triggers, hearing and neurological symptoms, medicines, vital signs, orthostatic readings, examination, ECG, laboratory results, cause, urgency, referral, driving advice, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
